name: Amr ibn Qurza
title: Shield of Wilayah
shortDescription: One of the brave Ansar who made himself a shield against enemy arrows on Ashura so no harm would reach Imam Hussain (AS).
fullStory:
Amr ibn Qurza al-Ansari was a sincere and devoted Shia in Kufa. His father was a companion of the Prophet (PBUH) and a supporter of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). Before the war started, Amr joined Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan from Kufa to be by his side.

On the day of Ashura, Amr sought permission from the Imam and entered the battlefield. Skilled in swordsmanship, he recited epic war poetry and charged into the heart of Umar Sa'd's army. After a long battle, he returned to the Imam.

One of the most beautiful manifestations of Amr's loyalty was when the enemy targeted Imam Hussain (AS) with their arrows. Amr immediately placed himself in front of the Imam, taking the arrows with his face and chest lest an arrow strike the pure body of the Master of Martyrs.

While his body was full of arrows and he was falling to the ground due to severe injuries, he turned to Imam Hussain (AS) and asked with a weak voice: 'O son of the Prophet! Have I fulfilled my pledge?' The Imam replied: 'Yes, you will be in Paradise before me. Send my greetings to the Messenger of God.'

Upon hearing this great glad tiding, Amr ibn Qurza attained martyrdom with a smile on his lips, immortalizing his name as the shield of Wilayah.
quoteArabic: أَوَفَيْتُ يا بْنَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ؟ قالَ: نَعَمْ، أَنْتَ أَمامِي فِي الجَنَّةِ
quote: (At martyrdom, he asked the Imam:) Have I fulfilled my pledge, O son of the Messenger of God? The Imam replied: Yes, you are ahead of me in Paradise.
